title: 2026 Park Vandalism Incidents
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: January 14, 2026
TO: Honorable Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Nathan Ullyot, Director, Parks and Recreation
Matt Brezina, Parks Manager
DEPT: Parks and Recreation
SUBJECT: Park Vandalism Update

The purpose of this memo is to inform City officials of a recent string of vandalism which occurred at multiple parks across the city over the past week. A combination of graffiti and structure damage were found by Park Rangers, Park Maintenance Staff, and City Security, contributing to significant labor and materials costs. Exhibit A contains photographs of the damage incurred.

Oxbow Nature Study Area Vandalism:
On Thursday, January 1, 2026, at approximately 3:35 a.m. surveillance video at Oxbow Nature Study Area captured footage of a vehicle running into the closed access gate at the entrance of Oxbow Nature Study Area. The impact bent the frame of the gate and damaged the locking mechanism. The incident was reported by City Security, and the gate was repaired by City staff on Monday, January 5, 2026.

Double Diamond Park Vandalism:
On Friday, January 1, 2026, park maintenance staff discovered damage to the restroom building at Double Diamond Park. It appears individuals attempted to gain access to the winterized and locked restroom units by damaging the doors. The locks were forced open and taped to prevent the locks relocking, the automatic closing mechanism and hinges were removed, and propane heaters were found inside the restroom building. Staff believe the restroom building was being used for temporary shelter. Park Maintenance staff secured the doors to prevent immediate access and are working on door modifications to prevent this type of vandalism in the future.

Horseman's Park Vandalism:
On the morning of January 2, 2026, Allied Security discovered a broken clothes donation box at Horseman's Park. The contents of the clothes donation box appeared to have been stolen reportedly by individuals in a brown Chevy Blazer. Allied Security was unable to obtain the Nevada license plate number as the brown Chevy Blazer drove off upon Allied Security's arrival. Staff will contact the donation center to address the damage.

Teglia's Paradise Park Restroom:
On Saturday, January 3, 2026, Park Rangers performed a routine inspection of the restroom near the playground in the northwest corner of Teglia's Paradise Park. Park Rangers found one of the two interior stalls severely burned by a fire. The fire appeared to have started on the baby changing station, which is a complete loss, and flames and smoke caused damage throughout the restroom unit. The rest of the interior amenities were unharmed, but significant decontamination and cleaning is needed before the restroom is usable again.

Barbara Bennett Park Graffiti:
During regular maintenance on Monday, January 5, 2026, staff found spray paint graffiti on the unpainted back side of the basketball court mural wall. An online police report was filed, and the Graffiti Abatement Team immediately painted over the spray paint graffiti.

Idlewild Park Graffiti:
During routine maintenance on Monday, January 5, 2026, staff found spray paint graffiti on the monument park sign near the east entrance of Idlewild Park. An online police report was filed, and Parks Maintenance staff immediately painted over the graffiti.

Exhibit A: Pictures of Graffiti and Vandalism

Oxbow Nature Study Gate Vandalism

Double Diamond Park Door Vandalism

Teglia's Paradise Park Restroom Fire

Barbara Bennett Park Wall Graffiti

Idlewild Park Sign Graffiti

fields.authors: Nathan Ullyot, Director, Parks and Recreation; Matt Brezina, Parks Manager
fields.tldr: Between January 1–5, 2026, vandalism occurred at six City of Reno parks, including gate damage, restroom break-ins, a restroom fire, theft from a donation box, and graffiti at two locations. Incidents were discovered by Park Rangers, Park Maintenance Staff, City Security, and Allied Security. Staff have made immediate repairs and filed police reports where applicable. Ongoing work includes door modifications at Double Diamond Park and decontamination of the burned restroom at Teglia's Paradise Park.
fields.key_stats: Six parks affected: Oxbow Nature Study Area, Double Diamond Park, Horseman's Park, Teglia's Paradise Park, Barbara Bennett Park, and Idlewild Park. Incidents spanned January 1–5, 2026. Damage types include gate collision, forced restroom entry, donation box theft, restroom fire (baby changing station a complete loss), and spray paint graffiti at two locations. Significant labor and materials costs noted but not quantified.
fields.key_decisions: Staff secured damaged doors at Double Diamond Park and are developing door modifications to prevent future forced entry. Graffiti at Barbara Bennett Park and Idlewild Park was immediately painted over. Staff will contact the donation center regarding the damaged clothes donation box at Horseman's Park.
fields.action_items: 1. Complete door modifications at Double Diamond Park restroom to prevent future forced entry. 2. Perform decontamination and cleaning of burned restroom at Teglia's Paradise Park before reopening. 3. Contact donation center to address damaged clothes donation box at Horseman's Park. 4. Online police reports filed for Barbara Bennett Park and Idlewild Park graffiti incidents.

dates: January 1, 2026 (approximately 3:35 a.m.) – Oxbow Nature Study Area gate vandalism
January 1, 2026 – Double Diamond Park restroom damage discovered
January 2, 2026 – Horseman's Park donation box vandalism discovered
January 3, 2026 – Teglia's Paradise Park restroom fire discovered
January 5, 2026 – Barbara Bennett Park graffiti discovered and painted over
January 5, 2026 – Idlewild Park graffiti discovered and painted over
people: Nathan Ullyot, Director, Parks and Recreation
